= Native Plugins

The `upstream` directory is a git submodule of
https://github.com/usetrmnl/plugins[usetrmnl/plugins], pinned by SHA. The
plugin code runs verbatim — never edit files under `upstream/` — so screens
render identically to TRMNL's hosted Core application. A submodule reference
(rather than vendored copies) also avoids redistributing the upstream code,
which carries no license file yet (its issue #9 indicates MIT is intended).

Clone with `--recurse-submodules`, or run `git submodule update --init`.

The `support` directory is Terminus-specific: it recreates the minimal Core
API surface the plugins expect (`Plugins::Base` with `fetch`/`post` HTTP
helpers, plugin settings, the user's time zone) plus an ERB renderer with the
Rails helpers used by views (`render`, `t`, `l`, number helpers,
`instance_name`, `Rails.application.credentials.*` backed by ENV). Where
upstream files can't run as-is (Ruby 4.0 syntax, missing helper files), the
loader applies load-time transforms — the upstream bytes stay untouched.
`weather` has no upstream Ruby at all; its locals provider lives in
`support/weather_plugin.rb` (keyless Open-Meteo).

== Syncing upstream

[source,bash]
----
git -C vendor/plugins/upstream pull origin master
git add vendor/plugins/upstream && git commit
----

Then re-run the smoke suites (`tmp/smoke-*.rb`) and add any new translation
keys the views use to `support/locales/en.yml` (Core's locale files are not
open source; missing keys fall back to a humanized form of the key).

== Status

See `STATUS.adoc` for the per-plugin support matrix and `OAUTH_DESIGN.adoc`
for the OAuth flow notes.
